📋 3-Stage Data Flow — fill what you have, come back later for the rest
Stage 1 — Quote (before advance): Project name, client, district, estimated length/width/area, cost. Enough to send a Quote.
Stage 2 — Survey (after advance paid): Field work — GPS coords, trees, muck points, land schedule, canopy density. Enough to generate JIR + Proposal.
Stage 3 — Post-approval: NPV UTR, sanction number, CA site details, pillar installation. Enough to generate SD docs.
Save early with just Stage 1 fields. Add Stage 2 data after survey. Don't wait for everything.
📝 Quote stage — fill only the fields below
After saving, when client accepts the quote, change Status field to "📋 Data Collection". Tab pages 2-5 (location, field survey, financial, alternatives) automatically unlock.

1Project Details (Stage 1 — required for Quote)

Location (for Proposal Documents)
👥 Inspection Team
Thumb rule: hill forest ≈ 80-120 trees/km (10m ROW) · plains ≈ 50-80 trees/km · dense stand ≈ 200+/km. Leave blank if you haven't visited site — Quote will show "Tree enumeration: TBD".
Project Classification
Expected Net Present Value payable. Used in the Selected vs Rejected cost comparison.
Expected compensatory afforestation cost. Used in comparison.
Client & Commercial (for your records)

These fields are private — they don't appear in any generated proforma. They help you track billing and client details.

10-digit number, no +91 prefix needed
Agreed fee for this proposal. Tracked on dashboard.

➕Extra Project Details ▾ (now distributed across pages 3–5)

📋 Land Schedule (Form 11) — per-type breakdown

⚠ Add a row for each land parcel. Same type can appear multiple times (e.g. two Reserved Forest plots with different khasras). Leave empty if no land of that type. Area auto-fills as you type Length × Width. Fill at site during joint inspection.

Land Type Length (m) Width (m) Area (ha)
Total Area: 0.0000 ha
🌳 Forest Density (for NPV, Form 38)
Tap 📐 Auto to estimate from your tree census + Land Schedule (forest area). Or pick manually. Controls NPV rate per ha in Form 38. UK Eco-class II: Very Dense ₹14.43L · Dense ₹11.56L · Open ₹7.22L per ha.
Forest Dept wants a polygon (area), not just a line. Width × length gives us the polygon. Typical: 6-7m hill road, 9-12m wider roads.
🔀 Rejected Alternative Alignment (प्रपत्र-9)

User agency surveys 2 options — the Selected (built) and one Rejected alternative. Both can be auto-filled from field data captured during JIR (toggle Active Alignment Session between Selected/Rejected when capturing coords + trees). Fields remain editable.

Selected: —
Rejected: No rejected data yet
💰 Financial Inputs (प्रपत्र-15.6, 34, 42, 45)
Total value of trees to be felled, as assessed by Forest Dept.
Market rate per hectare as per DM.
🏘️ Beneficiary Villages (प्रपत्र-33)

Villages that benefit from this project. The main village from Page 2 is added automatically — add more if multiple villages benefit.

Village name (ग्राम का नाम) Population Families
Tip: leave Population/Families blank if unknown.
Total beneficiaries: 0 people · 0 families
🖋️ Signatories / Officers Directory (प्रपत्रों के हस्ताक्षरकर्ता)

Just fill the officer's name for each role you need. The designation is the role itself (e.g. DFO = प्रभागीय वनाधिकारी), and the office is auto-suggested from your district / division / range / tehsil. Override Office only if your case is different. Leave blank for roles that don't sign your proforma.

Role (= designation) Name (नाम) Office (कार्यालय) — auto

ℹ Officer names auto-fill all proforma signature blocks AND letter recipients. No need to repeat anywhere else.

Chainage & Direction
⚠ If you walked/drove the alignment from the End side to the Start side, pick "Reverse" — maps, L-section, and KML will be flipped so Start is always on the left/beginning of drawings.

🏗Muck Disposal Points (Stage 2 — after site visit)

Forest Dept requires identification of muck disposal sites for BOTH alignments surveyed — the 🔴 Selected one (which will actually be built — goes into प्रपत्र-41) and the 🟢 Rejected alternative (for comparison in प्रपत्र-9). Each point becomes a polygon on the KML with calculated volume. The branch is auto-picked from the Active Alignment Session below. Fill this AFTER you visit the site — not at quote stage.

Captured: —

📍Active Alignment Session

Pick which alignment you're surveying before adding GPS points or trees. Every point and tree captured below will be auto-tagged with this branch.

Active session: —
✓ Alignment 1 data captured. Survey Alignment 2 (rejected alternative) as well?

2Branch Coordinates (Stage 2 — field survey)

GPS points marking the boundary or key locations. Tap "Capture Point" to add a new coordinate using your phone's GPS. Fill this during field survey visit.

3Trees & Land Types (Stage 2 — field enumeration)

Add each tree individually with species, diameter, and condition. Also capture land types encountered. This is the main field-survey activity — tree count is NOT known at quote stage.

📱 Mobile detected with Desktop Site ON
Chrome's "Desktop site" mode blocks high-accuracy GPS on Android. Untick it (Chrome menu ⋮ → "Desktop site") to capture proper field coordinates. Re-enable later if you want desktop-style layout for office work.
⚠ GPS & Camera Blocked
You seem to be viewing this file inside an in-app preview (Claude app, WhatsApp, Telegram, email viewer). These preview modes block GPS and camera for security.

To fix: Tap the ⋮ menu above → "Open in browser" OR download the file and open it in Chrome / Samsung Internet / Firefox. Everything will work fully there including GPS tracking, camera capture, and offline storage.

⚡Quick Actions

💾Local Storage

Generate the Joint Inspection Report (संयुक्त निरीक्षण रिपोर्ट) that is signed at the time of field inspection. Two versions are produced: Selected Alignment and Rejected Alignment. All data auto-fills from your saved record. After generating, you can edit any content directly in the browser before printing.

📋Create Joint Inspection Report

ℹWhat's in the Report

The Joint Inspection Report is a legally binding field document signed by all present officers (SDM, DFO/RO, Patwari, JE, Pradhan) at the time of inspection. It certifies that the joint inspection took place, lists the land types and trees observed, and either recommends the alignment or records the reasons for rejecting it.

Selected report contents: joint inspection certificate, land summary by category (आरक्षित वन / सिविल सोयम / वन पंचायत / नाप), tree count summary grouped by species and DBH class across land types, GPS coordinate table, and signature blocks for all inspection members.

Rejected report contents: same as selected but with an explicit rejection certificate and reason section. Used to document why this alignment was considered and rejected (required for Proforma 8/9).

After generating, the report opens in a new tab where every field becomes directly editable — click any text and type. Save as PDF or .doc when done.

Generate the complete forest land transfer proposal documents based on SOP for FC Act 1980. Pick a saved record and the app will produce all required proformas and the field signature document, ready to print, sign, and submit in triplicate.

📄Generate Proposal Documents

Select a saved record to generate: Proforma 4 (Joint Inspection), Proforma 10 (No Alternative Land), Proforma 11 (Land Schedule), Proforma 15 (Tree Schedule), Proforma 21 (Gram Sabha NOC), and the Field Signature Sheet.

Quick Presets
Loading proformas…

ℹHow to Use

Before site visit: Generate the Field Signature Sheet and print it. Take a physical copy with you.

During site visit: Fill coordinates and tree entries in the New Entry tab. Have the SDM, Forest Range Officer, Revenue officer, Ameen, JE, Gram Pradhan, and other representatives sign the printed Field Signature Sheet as each section of work is inspected.

After site visit: Tap Generate & Open. All proformas will be filled automatically with your collected data. Print in triplicate, collect physical signatures in blue ink, and submit to Divisional Forest Officer.

Case Monitoring System — 42-letter workflow for PMGSY / FCA 1980 forest-clearance cases. Tracks all letters from Stage 1 (Principled approval) through Stage 3i (final clearance). Uses the same Project Data as the proposal generator — no duplication.

📂Select Project

Letter tracking state is saved per-project. Each project has its own 42-letter pipeline.

📊Case Progress

⚠Pending > 60 days

✉Letters Pipeline

📝Case-specific Project Data

These fields are used only for Case Letters (not in proposals). Values persist per-project.

प्रेषण पंजिका / Dispatch Register. Track every letter sent — dispatch number, date, recipient, mode (by hand / post / email), current status, and days pending. Acts as your office's inward-outward register. When you mark any letter as "Sent" on the Letters tab, it auto-appears here.

📮Dispatch Register

Add Muck Disposal Point

Muck points for the alignment that will actually be built (Selected) vs. the alternative surveyed for comparison (Rejected). Both go into प्रपत्र-9 and प्रपत्र-41.
Photo will be auto-stamped with coordinates and date/time. Capture GPS first for location stamp.

New Dispatch Entry

Days after which the case is flagged as overdue if no reply received.

☁ Cloud Sync Setup

Set up a private Google Sheet as your cloud backup. One-time, ~5 minutes.

  1. Open Google Sheets: Tap sheets.new to create a new blank sheet. Name it "Forest Inspector Records".
  2. Open Apps Script: In the sheet → Extensions → Apps Script. A new tab opens with a code editor.
  3. Paste the backend: Delete the placeholder code. Tap the button below to copy the Apps Script code, then paste into the editor.
  4. Generate a secret key: In the editor's function dropdown (top toolbar) select generateSecretKey, then tap ▶ Run. Authorise when prompted. Click "Execution log" — copy the 32-character key.
    OR generate one here:
  5. Paste the key: Back in the script, replace CHANGE_ME_TO_A_LONG_RANDOM_STRING with your key (keep the quotes). Save the script (Ctrl-S).
  6. Deploy as Web App:
    • Click "Deploy" (top-right) → "New deployment"
    • Click the ⚙ gear icon → "Web app"
    • Description: "Forest Inspector sync"
    • Execute as: Me
    • Who has access: Anyone (security comes from the secret key)
    • Click Deploy → authorise → COPY the Web App URL
  7. Paste URL and key below in the form fields, tap "Test Connection". You should see ✓ green.
🔒 Privacy notes:
  • Your Google Sheet is private (visible only to your Google account)
  • The "Anyone" deployment doesn't make data public — it just lets the script accept HTTP calls. The secret key gates access.
  • Photos are NOT uploaded (kept locally to avoid storage limits). Records, trees, coords, GPS, signatories all sync.
  • Each phone keeps its own copy locally; the cloud is the merge point.

Enter Coordinates Manually

Use this when GPS is unavailable. You can copy coordinates from Google Maps, a paper survey, or another GPS device.

In Google Maps app: long-press a location → tap the coordinates at the top → Share → Copy. Then tap the button above to auto-fill.
Range: 28 to 32 for Uttarakhand (positive = North)
Range: 77 to 81 for Uttarakhand (positive = East)

✍️ Digital Signature

📋 Paste Trees from Excel

Copy rows from Excel/Google Sheets and paste below. The parser handles tab-separated (TSV) or comma-separated (CSV) data.

Expected Format — Click to expand
Columns (in this order):
Species | DBH (cm) | Condition | Land Type | Village/Notes

Example rows (tab/comma separated):
Chir Pine	20-30	green standing	आरक्षित वन भूमि	Gaw
Banj Oak	30-40	green standing	सिविल एवं सोयम	Gaw
Rhododendron	10-20	damaged	नाप	Kamsal
Tips:
  • Species can be English, Hindi, local, or scientific — auto-matched to database
  • DBH: exact value (e.g. 35) or range (e.g. 30-40)
  • Condition: green / dry / fallen / damaged / stump
  • Land Type: आरक्षित, सिविल, वन पंचायत, नाप, निजी (partial match works)
  • First header row auto-skipped if detected

📋 Paste GPS Points from Excel

Paste rows from Excel/Google Sheets with GPS data. Tab or comma separated.

Expected Format — Click to expand
Columns (in this order):
Point Name | Latitude | Longitude | Elevation (m) | Notes
Elevation and Notes are optional. If you put a header row with "Elevation" or "Altitude", the column order can be anything.

Example rows (with elevation):
Start Point	30.548001	79.125552	1840	
KM 2 ch. 1/2	30.548024	79.125539	1862	bend
Dump Yard 1	30.549002	79.126052	1875	forest land
Tips:
  • Latitude: 28 to 32 (for Uttarakhand)
  • Longitude: 77 to 82
  • Elevation in metres above sea level (range -500 to 9000) — needed for L-section drawing
  • UTM is auto-calculated from lat/lon
  • First header row auto-skipped
  • Also accepts DMS format like "30° 32' 53.23" N" (auto-converted)

Android GPS Fix
Android Chrome blocks GPS for local files

Choose any of these — fastest first:

🌍 Paste from Google Maps (works now)
  1. Open Google Maps app
  2. Long-press your location on the map (red pin appears)
  3. Tap the coordinates at the top of the screen, or tap Share → Copy
  4. Come back here, tap button below, then "📋 Paste from Google Maps"
⚡ Get GPS working: host on HTTPS (one-time, 30 sec)
  1. On PC: open app.netlify.com/drop
  2. Drag-drop this HTML file
  3. You get a https://xxx.netlify.app URL
  4. Open that URL on Android → GPS works ✅
